component: marriage x earnings joint (gate 2c pre-lock; who-marries-whom by earnings: assortative-mating contingency + earnings-conditional marriage hazards + around-event earnings dynamics + shared-earnings shape, from mh85_23 x the gate-1-certified earnings histories)
purpose: Gate-2c pre-lock evidence (v1): committed marriage x earnings joint reference moments, the 100-seed COUPLE-DISJOINT half-split noise floor (the sd basis the DRAFT tolerances derive from), the ratified 2a mean-over-K=20-draws scoring protocol adopted from the start, and the power-cap partition -- the first step of the gate_2c lock ceremony (gates.yaml gate_2c.lock_ceremony.required_before_any_2c_pass). Reads no gate and changes no gate on its own; writes NO gates.yaml block (the ceremony flip inserts the thresholds later). See draft_thresholds_note (NOT RATIFIED).

ceremony.step: 1 of the lock ceremony: the 100-seed split noise floor over the marriage x earnings joint (proposal); referee -> fixes -> verification -> ratify follow, and only the ratifying flip touches gates.yaml
ceremony.round_1_lessons_preempted: K=20 estimator adopted from the start (not the superseded single draw); estimand named as the SELECTED couple universe with join-coverage + marriage-decade disclosure; machine-recorded gated/report-only reasons; no perturbation dead zones in the binding tests; an always-runnable label-swap catch; raw MH7/MH8/MH12 mappings pre-verified against the staged formats (joinable-spouse count 32,522 reproduces the marriage-module figure)

data.marriage_history: populace_dynamics.data.marriage.marriage_history (mh85_23 Marriage History File): per-person marriages with joinable spouse_person_id (MH7 in 1-9308, MH8 in 1-399), start/end years and how_ended -- all decoded from the documented code sets and label-verified at read
data.earnings: populace_dynamics.data.family.family_earnings_panel (head/spouse family-file labor income, income years 1968-2022 -- annual 1968-1996, biennial 1998-2022; fix B, NOT the false round-1 '1993-2022') -> the NAWI indexing chain: the gate-2c earnings AXIS is the MEAN indexed positive-year earnings (fix C, a per-year earnings-capacity measure), NOT the round-1 career-sum AIME proxy. Levels are cohort-graded lower bounds; ranks/terciles/ratios are the gated quantities.
data.weights: each person carries the person-constant MOST-RECENT-POSITIVE PSID cross-sectional weight (populace_dynamics.data.panels.demographic_panel), exactly tranche 2a's weight_definition -- for EVERY gated family INCLUDING the six event-window cells (fix F). No unweighted gated statistic; the unweighted rate is reported alongside.
data.estimand: the marriage x earnings joint of the SELECTED universe of PSID couples with a computable per-year earnings axis for both partners -- NOT all PSID marriages. Named per governance.amendment_rules.description_claims_exactly_the_scored_surface (2b finding 3), and corrected to the true 1968-2022 surface (fix B). See selection_estimand and marriage_decade_slices.

selection_estimand.universe: directed couples = marriage-history records with a joinable PSID sample-member spouse where BOTH partners carry a computable per-year earnings axis (age-60 indexing year within the NAWI series [1951, 2035], and >= 5 observed positive-earnings years). Admission is unchanged from the round-1 draft; only the axis SCALAR (fix C) changed, so the SELECTED universe is identical.
selection_estimand.observed_window_facts: The family-file earnings span income years 1968-2022 (fix B, correcting the false '1993-2022'): 69% of the 14952 supply persons carry >=1 positive pre-1993 year (their pre-1993 dollar share averages ~68%); observed positive years run median 13 / p90 28 / max 42 (a 1993-2022 window would allow ~17). Truncation is COHORT-GRADED: a 1940s-born ego is observed near-fully (annual 1968-1996), a post-1970-born ego is heavily truncated -- the round-1 'levels are uniform lower bounds' reading inverts by cohort.
selection_estimand.note: The earnings-axis requirement selects birth cohorts whose age-60 year lands in the NAWI window and whose careers overlap the 1968-2022 family-earnings panel; the couples therefore concentrate in older marriage decades. This selection is the 2c analogue of gate 2b's era-weighting disclosure -- named, not implied.

marriage_decade_slices.note: REPORT-ONLY, never gated. Per-marriage-decade couple counts, the weighted concordant-tercile (diagonal) share, and the within-couple earnings-axis Spearman rho (with the round-1 career-AIME-proxy rho alongside for the fix-C decomposition), so the SELECTED marriage-era mixture the pooled contingency certifies is visible: a candidate matching only one era can be read against the pooled cells it is scored on. The per-year axis restores sensible increasing positive sorting across decades; the career-AIME proxy was NEGATIVE in 6 of 8 decades (a cross-decade composition artifact of the observation mechanics -- see assortative_correlation.decomposition).

assortative_correlation_report_only.statistic: within-couple Spearman rank correlation of the per-year indexed earnings axis (with the career-AIME-proxy decomposition)

decomposition.note: Fix C construct-validity decomposition. The gated earnings axis is the per-year mean indexed earnings (earnings_axis_spearman ~0.49, literature-scale within-couple sorting). The round-1 career-sum AIME proxy correlated only ~0.12 cross-sex: cross-sex tercile pooling halves it (within-sex rank ~0.21) and the observed-positive-years count anti-correlates within couples (~ -0.10, single-earner-era head/wife role-years), which the zeros-filled career sum rewards. The proxy's split-half rank reliability ~0.98, so the weak career-AIME signal is NOT sampling noise -- it is what the career-sum proxy measures, which is why the axis is the per-year measure.

assortative_correlation_report_only.note: REPORT-ONLY: a rank correlation is bounded in [-1, 1] and can cross zero, so it is not a scale-free |ln ratio| and is never gated. The assortative-mating GATE runs on the tercile contingency shares (assort_mating.own{o}_spouse{u}) over the per-year earnings axis; these rhos and the decomposition are the interpretable summary reported alongside.

panel_construction.couple_unit: directed couple = one marriage-history record (ego -> spouse). A two-sample-member marriage yields two directed records ONLY when both partners have a marriage record naming the other; the contingency is NOT symmetric by construction (fix H): some records are one-directional and some mirrored pairs disagree on start_year -- see directed_couple_record_accuracy. The effective independent unit is the couple, priced by the couple-disjoint floor.
panel_construction.split_unit: couple-graph CONNECTED COMPONENT (component_id; fix A). Union-find over the directed-couple edges groups both members of every couple (and any earnings/marital chain of shared persons) into one component <= 4 persons; the 50/50 split picks components, so no couple straddles. Person-level families (hazards, event windows) are unaffected. The floor split and the candidate holdout commitment are the SAME object.
panel_construction.couple_disjoint_split: Fix A (round-1 finding 1). Round-1 split person-disjoint by EGO, so a couple whose two members split to opposite sides put one directed record on EACH half (measured seed 0: 50.4% of mirrored pairs straddled). The two halves shared the very unit the tranche certifies, understating the realized sigma 1.2-1.46x on the assortative diagonals (own2_spouse2 ~1.46x, own3_spouse3 ~1.31x, own1_spouse1 ~1.21x) and all four shared-earnings cells. The component split removes it. BOTH directions of the round-1 disclosure: the ego-split tolerances were too TIGHT on those cells, and the OC disclosed 0.95/0.9774 there was 0.83/0.80 at couple-level noise -- this couple-disjoint floor re-derives the tolerances, partition and OC honestly (expect diagonal widening and demotions at the cap).
panel_construction.earnings_axis_semantics: The earnings axis (fix C) = mean NAWI-indexed positive-year earnings over the PSID-observed years (1968-2022), a per-year earnings-capacity measure; terciles / combined-level cutpoints are fixed on the full earnings supply and applied to every half-split so the categories do not shift. It REPLACES the round-1 career-sum AIME proxy, whose within-couple sorting (~0.12) was dominated by observation mechanics; the per-year axis sorts ~0.49 (literature-scale). LEVELS are cohort-graded lower bounds; the gated quantities are terciles / ranks / cutpoint ratios.
panel_construction.hazard_stratifier_caveat: the marriage-hazard 'own earnings tercile' is the person's FIXED per-year earnings-axis tercile, not contemporaneous earnings, so it summarises earnings CAPACITY (with the usual marriage<->earnings reverse-causality caveat), used as a stable stratum for the age-band hazard
panel_construction.event_window_convention: the around-event earnings ratio is (mean post-window earnings) / (mean pre-window earnings) over a +/-3-year window, DETRENDED by the non-event placebo drift deflator (fix E); an event contributes only where the person has >=1 observed positive-earnings year in BOTH windows -- an earnings-support restriction. Support windows overlap pre-1993 for a substantial share of events (the panel is 1968-2022, fix B).
coverage_caveats: the earnings source is family-file HEAD/SPOUSE labor income only, so a person never observed as head or spouse has no earnings axis and cannot enter a couple

the earnings axis is a truncated partial career (1968-2022 observed years, cohort-graded truncation; fix B), so its LEVELS understate lifetime earnings; the gated moments use terciles / ranks / ratios
the split is couple-disjoint by component (fix A), so no couple straddles the two halves
boundary fragility: a few cells' tolerances sit near T_max=ln(1.5) and could cross under re-measure (boundary_fragility bootstrap); the lock freezes the committed partition

directed_couple_record_accuracy.note: Fix H record correction. The contingency is NOT 'symmetric by construction': a two-sample-member marriage yields two directed records only when BOTH partners have a marriage record that names the other; measured, some records are one-directional (spouse-has-no-record or never-names-ego) and some mirrored pairs disagree on start_year, so the decade slices and off-diagonal cells are direction-dependent. The effective independent unit is the couple (priced by the couple-disjoint floor, fix A).

statistic_families.assort_mating: own-tercile x spouse-tercile contingency share over directed couples (3x3), on the per-year earnings axis (fix C)
statistic_families.first_marriage_by_earnings: first-marriage hazard by earnings-axis tercile x age band x sex (never-married person-years at risk)
statistic_families.remarriage_by_earnings: remarriage hazard by earnings-axis tercile x sex (post-dissolution person-years at risk)
statistic_families.earnings_around_marriage: placebo-DETRENDED weighted median (post/pre) earnings ratio around marriage, by sex + all (fix E: net of generic drift)
statistic_families.earnings_around_divorce: placebo-DETRENDED weighted median (post/pre) earnings ratio around divorce, by sex + all (fix E: net of generic drift)
statistic_families.shared_earnings_ratio: adjacent-quintile cutpoint ratios of the couple's combined earnings axis (scale-free distribution shape)

external_anchor.note: No external marriage x earnings anchor is bundled with this STEP-1 floor. The reference moments (assortative-mating contingency, earnings-conditional marriage hazards, around-event earnings dynamics, shared-earnings shape) are cross-checked only against themselves (real-vs-real half-split). This is a KNOWN GAP vs tranche 2a (which shipped concept-decomposed NCHS anchors in its floor PR). It is declared, not fabricated. As with gate 2b, the concept-decomposed external report (candidate sources below) must PRECEDE the ratifying flip -- a later ceremony step than this floor.
external_anchor.concept_delta: The couple earnings axis is the PSID-observed mean indexed positive-year earnings (family-file labor income, income years 1968-2022, NAWI-indexed, averaged over observed positive years; fix C) -- a per-year earnings-capacity measure, NOT the true SSA covered-earnings AIME, so LEVELS are not level-comparable to SSA/OCACT tabulations without a truncation decomposition. The partial-career truncation is COHORT-GRADED (worst for post-1970 births; fix B), not a blanket lower bound. The gated moments use terciles, within-couple ranks and cutpoint RATIOS, robust to that truncation. The future anchor must bridge to the per-year sorting (within-couple earnings rank ~0.49), NOT the round-1 career-sum proxy rho ~0.12 (an observation-mechanics artifact; see assortative_correlation.decomposition).
candidate_sources: Census/ACS PUMS spouse-earnings correlation by cohort
CPS ASEC married-couple earnings-rank contingency tables
Schwartz & Mare (2005) / Eika-Mogstad-Zafar assortative-mating series (educational + earnings sorting)

internal_noise_floor.method: COUPLE-DISJOINT 50/50 half-split (fix A: populace_dynamics.harness.panel.split_panel_by_person on attrs.component_id, fraction=0.5, seeds 0-99) of every reference-moment cell; the floor statistic is |ln(rate_A / rate_B)| between two COUPLE-DISJOINT real halves (both members of every couple on one side, so the halves are genuinely independent) -- the sd basis the DRAFT gate-2c thresholds derive from as round(mean + k*sd). The round-1 ego split was NOT couple-disjoint (halves shared straddling couples, understating the diagonal/shared sigma 1.2-1.46x).

protocol.estimator: mean-over-K=20-draws (tranche 2a amendment 1, ratified 2026-07-08, PR 96). The candidate cell rate rbar_candidate,s is the MEAN over K=20 pre-registered simulation draws (numpy default_rng(5200 + k), k=0..19; a stream DISTINCT from the split seeds), scored ONCE per cell as |ln(rbar_candidate,s / rate_a,s)| -- NOT the mean of the per-draw |ln| scores. This 2c build uses the ratified 2a estimator FROM THE START; the superseded single-draw replicate the 2b round-1 referee flagged (finding 1) never enters.
protocol.statistic: |ln(rbar_candidate,s / rate_a,s)|, rbar = mean over K=20 draws of the simulated cell rate; symmetric and scale-free
protocol.description: Per gate seed s: refit the candidate on the seed-s train complement (side B; the seed-s holdout persons excluded from all fitting), SIMULATE side A's couples / marital events / earnings at K=20 pre-registered draws (default_rng(5200 + k)), form the 20-draw mean cell rate rbar_candidate,s, and score |ln(rbar / rate_a)| against side A's own empirical rate. The real-vs-real half-vs-half floor |ln(rate_A/rate_B)| is the null. Seed s passes iff every gated cell holds; the gate passes iff >=4 of 5 gate seeds pass.
protocol.varies_per_seed: the 50/50 COUPLE-DISJOINT split by couple-graph component (fix A; holdout = side A of split_panel_by_person(component_id, seed=s)); the candidate's fit complement and its K=20 simulated draws of the holdout
protocol.scored_against: the seed-s holdout half's empirical rate (rate_a in noise_floor_per_seed)
protocol.conjunction: all gated cells per seed AND >=4 of 5 gate seeds

candidate_construction.couple_emission: DIRECTED records per holdout ego: for each holdout-side couple emit one record per ego, and BOTH directions (ego A -> spouse B AND ego B -> spouse A) when both partners are holdout egos -- the reference is symmetrized-directed and a single-orientation (each couple once, one sex as ego) emission is NON-CONFORMANT (measured 6/9 assort cells beyond tolerance, up to 4.9x).
candidate_construction.earnings_axis: the per-year mean indexed positive-year earnings (fix C), the both-axis selection applied to the SIMULATED careers: a couple enters only where BOTH partners are admitted (age-60 indexing year in the NAWI series and >= MIN_EARNINGS_YEARS observed positive years) on the PSID observation grid.
candidate_construction.observation_grid: simulate earnings on the PSID family-file observation grid (annual income years 1968-1996, biennial 1998-2022; family-file income refers to the prior year), which defines which years are 'observed', the MIN_EARNINGS_YEARS admission on that grid, and the NAWI-window birth filter -- nominal dollars, NOT real.
candidate_construction.weight: the demographic most-recent-positive PSID weight for every gated family INCLUDING the event windows (fix F).
candidate_construction.event_window_support_and_detrend: earnings_around_* uses only events with an observed positive year in BOTH +/-3y sub-windows, and the emitted statistic is the post/pre median NET of the committed placebo deflator (fix E), so a real-terms candidate is scored on the event increment, not nominal drift.
candidate_construction.note: Fix D (round-1 finding 4). The protocol pins draws / stream / shape / invalidation; this block pins the CANDIDATE-side couple universe and orientation the round-1 draft left implicit.

protocol.adopted: the estimator is tranche 2a amendment 1 (amendment_history entry 1, ratified 2026-07-08 by merge of PR 96), adopted here at draft time so the disclosed OC is achievable and the single-draw defect never enters (unlike the 2b draft, which shipped the superseded text and the referee flagged it).
protocol.basis_note: The faithful-candidate OC (p_seed_pass 0.9641, p_gate_pass_4_of_5 0.988) is computed on a DRAW-NOISE-FREE half-normal basis: a faithful candidate's per-cell score ~ half-normal(realized_sigma) -- floor sigmas only, no simulation-draw term. The mean-over-K=20-draws estimator SHARES that basis (the 20-draw mean rate approximates the draw-noise-free expected rate, shrinking the residual simulation term ~1/sqrt(K)), so the OC is achievable. Under a SINGLE-draw estimator it would be UNACHIEVABLE (tranche 2a amendment-1 finding 1): one simulation replicate injects ~one extra floor-sigma per cell (score ~sqrt(1.5) x realized_sigma), roughly halving P(seed). This 2c build adopts the ratified K=20 estimator FROM THE START, so the single-draw defect the 2b round-1 referee flagged never enters.

fresh_run_artifact_schema.applies_to: a fresh candidate one-shot run registered AFTER a future gate_2c lock; NEVER this floor build, which measures the real-vs-real half-vs-half null (no simulation, no draws).

per_draw_per_cell_rates.rule: commit every draw's per-cell rate r[k, cell, s] for all K=20 draws (numpy default_rng(5200 + k), k=0..19), all 27 gated cells, and all 5 gate seeds -- NOT only the 20-draw means. So rbar_candidate,s = mean over k recomputes cell-by-cell and |ln(rbar / rate_a)| is independently auditable, not taken on trust.

undefined_draw_rule.rule: if any gated cell's rate is UNDEFINED on any draw (empty simulated denominator), the RUN IS INVALIDATED and must be re-registered and re-run. No draw may be dropped, skipped, substituted, or re-rolled; rbar is always the mean over ALL K registered draws.
undefined_draw_rule.rationale: silently dropping an undefined draw reintroduces the post-hoc draw selection the mean-over-all-K rule forecloses; a smaller outcome-selected set is not the pre-registered mean-of-20.

commit.per_cell_per_draw_sd: the sd across the K=20 draws of each gated cell's rate, per gated cell per gate seed -- the per-draw over-dispersion the 20-draw mean hides.
commit.max_per_draw_abs_ln_per_cell: the maximum over the K=20 draws of |ln(r[k, cell, s] / rate_a,s)| per gated cell per gate seed -- the worst single-draw excursion, reported alongside rbar.

per_draw_dispersion_disclosure.note: REPORT-ONLY: no dispersion cap gates the run; the disclosure lets a referee see whether a passing 20-draw mean conceals a wild individual draw.

knobs.note: The load-bearing judgment knobs, committed so the derivations tier can equality-pin them against the builder / module constants (fix G closes the CI-blind knob gaps: MIN_EARNINGS_YEARS, AIME_TERCILE_QUANTILES, EVENT_WINDOW_YEARS, the draw-stream base, the split fraction).

event_window_placebo.construction: The placebo deflator is the weighted median post/pre ratio over EVERY qualifying non-event +/-3y window (centre year >= 4 years from ANY of the person's marital events, positive-earnings support both sides), each person's windows sharing the person's demographic weight so each person contributes once. It measures the generic nominal life-cycle + inflation drift a window on this 1968-2022 panel shows with NO event (measured ~1.20). It is applied as a fixed panel-level deflator (like a price index), identical across every half-split, so it cancels in the |ln ratio| floor and the event-window tolerances are unchanged from the raw medians.
event_window_placebo.note: Fix E (round-1 finding 5). Round-1 gated the RAW nominal ratios (marriage.all 1.27 / divorce.all 1.26), ~77% of whose log magnitude is generic drift a placebo window shows with no event -- so a real-terms candidate failed by construction and a zero-effect + extra-drift candidate passed. Detrending scores the event increment. The F/M asymmetry the family really carries is preserved (marriage male > female; divorce female > male).

boundary_fragility.method: B=5000 bootstrap resamples of each cell's 100 committed floor values; tolerance recomputed as round(mean + k*sd, r) per resample; p(cross T_max=ln(1.5)). Event-eligible cells only. Deterministic (numpy default_rng(20260710)).
boundary_fragility.note: Fix H(ii) boundary-fragility disclosure (round-1 wart 12 quantified). A few tolerances sit near the ln(1.5) cap and could cross under re-measure; the lock freezes the committed partition. Disclosure, not defect.

pooled_age_power.note: Fix H(i) record correction. Round-1 said pooling the sparse late-first-marriage age bands would MASK a standalone-gateable 35-44 cell. Measured, NO pooled t{o}.35+|sex aggregate is gate-eligible either (tolerances all above T_max=ln(1.5)); the no-aggregates CHOICE is right but the honest reason the sparse cells are report-only is POWER, not masking.

aggregations_note: EMPTY by design: gate-2c declares no coverage-recovery aggregates. The no-aggregates CHOICE is right, but the honest reason the sparse late-first-marriage cells stay report-only is POWER, not masking (fix H record correction): NO pooled t{o}.35+|sex aggregate is standalone-gateable either -- measured 100-seed tolerances [0.634, 1.265], all above T_max=ln(1.5) (see pooled_age_power). Round-1's 'would mask a standalone-gateable 35-44 cell' rationale was counterfactual.

holdout_ids.numpy_generator: populace_dynamics.harness.panel.split_panel_by_person on attrs.component_id (fix A, couple-disjoint): side A (holdout) = the persons whose component c of np.sort(unique component_id) has np.random.default_rng(seed).random(n_components)[c] < 0.5; both members of every couple share a component, so the holdout is couple-coherent. The committed sha256 is over the holdout's ego person_ids.

training_copy_check.candidate: verbatim copy of the train half (side B rates)
training_copy_check.interpretation: A train-copy passes at ~the noise floor (max score below 1x tolerance), as any moment gate's copy must. The marriage x earnings cells do not fix this -- a copy reproduces every moment; the memorisation defence is procedural (registration + holdout exclusion + no_self_rescue), NOT the cell set. What the cells DO catch is non-copy structural failure: a couple model that mismatches who marries whom by earnings tercile, the earnings-conditional marriage timing, the around-event earnings dynamics, or the shared-earnings distribution shape.

faithful_candidate_oc.method: Independence-approx normal OC. Per gated cell a faithful candidate's score ~ half-normal(realized_sigma); cell pass = 2*Phi(tolerance/sigma)-1. Seed pass = product over gated cells; gate = P(>=4 of 5 seeds) = p^5 + 5 p^4 (1-p). The K=20 estimator shares this draw-noise-free basis (see protocol.basis_note).

draft_thresholds.note: DRAFT per-cell |ln ratio| tolerances = round(floor mean + 4*sd, 3); gated cells only (defined on all seeds AND >=20 events AND tolerance <= T_max). NOT yet mirrored into gates.yaml -- the gate_2c lock-ceremony flip inserts them after the referee + verification rounds.

draft_thresholds_note:
DRAFT GATE-2C VALIDATION EVIDENCE -- PRE-LOCK, NOT RATIFIED (v1, the first step of the gate-2c lock ceremony; the 2b round-1 referee's lessons pre-empted, not repeated).

Pre-lock evidence package for the gate-2c marriage x earnings JOINT statistics (who marries whom, and what they earn; gates.yaml gate_2c: mh85_23 marital histories crossed with the gate-1-certified earnings histories), the analogue of what preceded tranche 2a's and gate 2b's lock, one tranche over. It changes no locked value and no model reads it. It writes NO gates.yaml block: gate_2c already exists as an unlocked stub, and the lock-ceremony flip (proposal -> referee -> fixes -> verification -> ratify) inserts the thresholds later. The k value (4) is a DRAFT proposal, for the ceremony to fix.

PROTOCOL (option a, the ratified 2a K=20 estimator -- adopted from the START). Per gate seed s a candidate is refit on the seed-s TRAIN complement (side B), SIMULATES the holdout couples/events at K=20 pre-registered draws (numpy default_rng(5200 + k), a stream distinct from the split seeds), and each cell is scored ONCE as |ln(rbar_candidate,s / rate_a,s)| where rbar is the mean over the 20 draws of the cell RATE (NOT the mean of the per-draw |ln| scores), against the seed-s HOLDOUT half's own empirical rate. The symmetric half-vs-half floor is the null. The candidate-side couple universe / orientation is pinned in protocol.candidate_construction (fix D: directed both-orientation emission, per-year earnings axis on the PSID observation grid, placebo-detrended event windows). Seed passes iff every gate-eligible cell holds; the gate passes iff >=4 of 5 seeds pass. The single-draw text the 2b round-1 referee flagged (finding 1) is pre-empted; see protocol.basis_note.

INTERNAL FLOOR (fix A: COUPLE-DISJOINT). The 50/50 half-split by couple-graph CONNECTED COMPONENT (component_id), not ego person_id, |log rate ratio| floor over 100 split seeds -- so both members of every couple land on one side and the halves are genuinely independent. The round-1 ego split let 50.4% of mirrored pairs straddle, understating the assortative-diagonal and shared-earnings floor sigma 1.2-1.46x. Each DRAFT per-cell tolerance is round(mean + 4*sd, 3); on 100 seeds the estimator is stable. The faithful-candidate operating characteristic recomputes P(seed) = 0.9641 and P(gate >=4/5) = 0.988 over 27 gated cells, draw-noise-free, achievable under the K=20 mean. (Round-1 disclosed 0.95 / 0.9774 at the ego-split floor, which was 0.83 / 0.80 at couple-level noise; this couple-disjoint floor prices that honestly.)

POWER CAP. A cell is gate-eligible only if its stabilised tolerance <= T_max = ln(1.5) (a gated cell accepts at most a 1.5x rate error) AND it carries >=20 events on the weaker half. The sparse late-first-marriage-by-earnings cells (35-44 / 45+ terciles) and the noisiest median cells fail the cap and stay report-only; the partition is derived from cell_stability, never hand-picked. There are NO coverage-recovery aggregates -- and no pooled 35+ cell is standalone-gateable either (measured; see pooled_age_power), so the honest reason is POWER, not masking (fix H).

ESTIMAND / SELECTION (fix B/C -- named, not implied; description matches the scored surface). The gated moments describe the SELECTED universe of PSID couples with a computable per-year earnings axis for BOTH partners: 7994 directed couples (4078 unordered pairs) from 32522 joinable marriage records -- join coverage 0.2458 of joinable records, 0.553 of earnings-axis-computable egos. It is NOT the marriage x earnings joint of all PSID marriages. The family-file earnings span income years [1968, 2022] (1968-2022, NOT the false round-1 '1993-2022'): 69% of supply persons carry pre-1993 years, observed positive years run median 13 / max 42, and the truncation is cohort-graded. See selection_estimand, marriage_decade_slices and the WARTS.

EARNINGS AXIS (fix C -- construct validity). The gated axis is the per-year mean indexed positive-year earnings (within-couple rank ~0.49, literature-scale sorting), NOT the round-1 career-sum AIME proxy (rank ~0.12, dominated by observation mechanics: observed-year counts anti-correlate -0.10, cross-sex pooling halves it, split-half reliability 0.98). Terciles / cutpoints are fixed on the full earnings supply and applied to every half-split. The contingency over directed couples is NOT symmetric by construction (fix H: some records one-directional, some mirrors disagree on start_year); the within-couple Spearman rho + decomposition is REPORT-ONLY.

EVENT WINDOWS (fix E -- drift-corrected). earnings_around_* is the post/pre median NET of the non-event placebo drift deflator (~1.20), so the gate certifies the event increment, not the ~77% generic nominal drift; a real-terms candidate is no longer failed by construction. See event_window_placebo.

EXTERNAL ANCHOR. NONE bundled (external_anchor.status = none_bundled) -- a KNOWN GAP vs 2a; declared not fabricated, and REQUIRED before the ratifying flip (finding 5).

GATE-ELIGIBLE VS REPORT-ONLY. GOVERNANCE. One-shot outer runs, amendments only by public proposal + referee round, no_self_rescue and the version pin inherited from gate 1; the per-seed holdout ids are committed (holdout_ids); every gated statistic -- INCLUDING the event windows (fix F) -- is weighted by the person-constant most-recent positive PSID weight (no unweighted gated statistic). The earnings-axis chain pins pe_us_revision (classifier_version_pin). BASELINE: these statistics feed spousal / survivor benefit LEVELS through who marries whom by earnings; this evidence fixes the component's estimation/validation standard only, not a scored reform.

revision_pins.build_commit_note: The build script + module that produced this artifact live on branch gate2c-floors and are committed TOGETHER with this file; a git commit cannot embed its own hash (chicken-and-egg), so no field names the build commit. base_sha pins the origin/master commit the branch extends; pe_us_revision pins the SSA-parameter checkout the AIME chain used (classifier_version_pin). To reproduce: check out branch gate2c-floors HEAD (NOT base_sha) with the same pe_us_revision and run .venv/bin/python scripts/build_gate2c_floors.py -- it reproduces this artifact bit-identically except elapsed_seconds and the two sha pins.
